name: Metaconceptual Art Knowledge Graph
description: Canonical, machine-readable knowledge graph for Metaconceptual Art. Each node carries a stable site ID and, where it corresponds to a real concept, person, or movement, a Wikidata QID plus the art-domain authority IDs (Getty ULAN for people, Getty AAT for concepts, Grove Art Online) so the museum connects outward to both the general and the art-specific linked-data graph. The works are also published as a set of CIDOC-CRM-correct Linked Art (API 1.0) records — digital objects, a textual work, a group, a concept, a collection set, and a provenance activity — each with a HAL _links block and served as application/ld+json. The dataset distinguishes origin from publication: Sun & Rain Works was established in 2005, Metaconceptual Art was created as a movement in 2007, 2009 receipts and metadata are treated as early-work evidence, and 2026 is the public linked-data publication layer.

linkedArtRecords.label: Construction of the Museum as Concept
Sun & Rain Works' auction engagement (Sotheby's Photographs, 2009)
Evidence archive: Sun & Rain Works auction engagement (2009)
Sun & Rain Works' conceptual claim to art at auction (2009)

groveArtNote: Grove Art Online IDs (Oxford) are recorded for reference; the resource is subscription-gated, so the site links to the open Getty vocab URIs.

prefixes.source: Person, text, or origin cited by the work
prefixes.role: A position activated within the system

nodes.label: The Website Itself
nodes.description: The public page treated as the artwork: a surface where language, navigation, metadata, revision, and viewing conditions become material.

nodes.label: Eight Sentences On Metaconceptual Art
nodes.description: A sentence sequence after Sol LeWitt framing Metaconceptual Art as a field of form, institution, market, history, medium, access, and life.

nodes.label: www.wtfisart.com
nodes.description: A born-digital Metaconceptual Art work: a website built and owned by Sun & Rain Works around 2009, associated by the studio with the conceptual work The Auction as Artwork.

nodes.label: Construction of the Museum as Concept
nodes.description: The museum appears inside the artwork as something still being built: institutional authority shown as architecture, ornament, labor, and frame.

nodes.label: Art Market Reform
nodes.description: An image study that treats art, price, reform, and institutional display as one unstable field.

nodes.label: Eightfold Sprocket
nodes.description: A diagrammatic emblem for the eight-sentence proposition set: repetition, rotation, and system held in a compact visual sign.

nodes.label: Movement Graph
nodes.description: The knowledge graph treated as an artwork: relations, authorities, claims, and viewers arranged as a walkable system.

nodes.label: Proof of Movement: Metaconceptual Art
nodes.description: A citable linked-open-data record defining the evidence standard for Metaconceptual Art as a natural, credible, and valid movement.

nodes.label: The Auction as Artwork
nodes.description: A Metaconceptual Art work that Sun & Rain Works designates in connection with its 2009 Sotheby's Photographs auction engagement (sale N08533), where it consigned an Ansel Adams photograph (lot 16). A readymade-logic designation asserted by the artist, not a witnessed live-creation event; the catalogue does not name Sun & Rain Works. Two associated works: www.wtfisart.com and WTF is Ansel Adams.

nodes.label: WTF is Ansel Adams
nodes.description: A Metaconceptual Art work Sun & Rain Works designates around its consignment of the Ansel Adams photograph (lot 16) to Sotheby's Photographs, 2009 — a conceptual designation asserted by the artist, not a witnessed performance. Companion to www.wtfisart.com.

nodes.label: Evidence archive: auction engagement (2009)
nodes.description: A record describing the private archive Sun & Rain Works holds for its 2009 Sotheby's engagement — 2008 correspondence, the consignment of an Ansel Adams photograph (lot 16), domain/hosting receipts for www.wtfisart.com, photographs, and metadata. These evidence the artist identity, the engagement, and the website's date; they do not record a witnessed live-creation event. Independently verifiable: the public catalogue lot (N08533).

nodes.label: Conceptual claim to art at auction (2009)
nodes.description: The artist's asserted conceptual designation: that Sun & Rain Works framed its 2009 Sotheby's auction engagement as art. Presented as the artist's position, not an adjudicated historical fact; makes no 'first in history' claim and disputes no later artist or event.

nodes.label: Metaconceptual Art
nodes.description: A self-defined movement concept created by Sun & Rain Works in 2007: art that treats art, its institutions, markets, archives, viewers, metadata, and provenance as the material of the work.

nodes.label: Conceptual art
nodes.description: Contemporary art movement that shifts attention from the handmade object to the idea.

nodes.label: Institutional critique
nodes.description: Artistic practice that treats the museum, market, gallery, and frame as the material of the work.

nodes.label: Systems art
nodes.description: Art influenced by cybernetics and systems theory, foregrounding relation, process, and feedback over the discrete object.

nodes.label: Internet art
nodes.description: Art that uses the website, protocol, page, and browser as its artistic site.

nodes.label: Found object / readymade
nodes.description: Art created from undisguised, often modified, objects designated as art by context rather than craft.

nodes.label: Provenance
nodes.description: The traceable chronology of ownership, custody, and lineage of an object, idea, or claim.

nodes.label: Knowledge graph
nodes.description: An information repository structured as a graph of entities and relationships, the form the meta-museum takes.

nodes.label: Semantic Web
nodes.description: Extension of the Web that enables data exchange through shared identifiers, vocabularies, and machine-readable relations.

nodes.label: Linked open data
nodes.description: Linked data available under an open license, used here as the public evidence medium for the movement's claims.

nodes.label: Sol LeWitt
nodes.description: American artist (1928-2007); his Sentences on Conceptual Art establish the sentence as a conceptual, rule-based unit.

nodes.label: Joseph Kosuth
nodes.description: American conceptual artist (born 1945); art as inquiry into the conditions of art.

nodes.label: Marcel Duchamp
nodes.description: French artist (1887-1968); designation, context, and the readymade.

nodes.label: Sotheby's Photographs auction (sale N08533, 2009)
nodes.description: The public auction (sale N08533), carried out by Sotheby's, at which Sun & Rain Works consigned an Ansel Adams photograph (lot 16) and which it designates as the material of the conceptual work The Auction as Artwork; www.wtfisart.com and WTF is Ansel Adams are associated works.

nodes.label: Sotheby's
nodes.description: International auction house (founded 1744) named as the institution that carried out the 2009 Photographs auction (sale N08533). Recorded as context only, with no assertion of authorship, endorsement, or dispute.

nodes.label: Provenance Layer
nodes.description: Tracks authorship, revision, and the lineage of every claim and work.

nodes.label: Viewer
nodes.description: The position that activates meaning by reading, querying, and interpreting the work.

nodes.label: Wikidata Layer
nodes.description: Grounded the semantic layer in Wikidata identifiers and added a live related-concepts query.

nodes.label: Origin Provenance
nodes.description: Machine-readable chronology distinguishing Sun & Rain Works formation in 2005, Metaconceptual Art movement creation in 2007, 2009 early-work evidence, and 2026 linked-data publication.

nodes.label: Recommended Citation
nodes.description: Makes the work formally referenceable.
